entries.title: On 2 Corinthians 11:21
entries.excerpt: Yet whereinsoever any is bold, (I speak in foolishness,) I am bold also. See him again drawing back and using depreciation and correctives beforehand, although he has already even said many such things: Would that you could bear with me in a little foolishness; 2 Corinthians 11:1 and again, Let no man think me foolish: if you do, yet as foolish receive me. 2 Corinthians 11:16 That which I speak, I speak not after the Lord, but as in foolishness. 2 Corinthians 11:17 Seeing that many glory after the flesh, I will glory also; 2 Corinthians 11:18 and here again, Whereinsoever any is bold, (I…
